vulnerabilities.title: Denial of Service in uap-core when processing crafted User-Agent strings
vulnerabilities.description:
### Impact

Some regexes are vulnerable to regular expression denial of service (REDoS) due to overlapping capture groups. This allows remote attackers to overload a server by setting the User-Agent header in an HTTP(S) request to maliciously crafted long strings.

### Patches

Please update uap-core to &amp;gt;= v0.7.3

Downstream packages such as uap-python, uap-ruby etc which depend upon uap-core follow different version schemes.

vulnerabilities.title: Denial of Service in uap-core
vulnerabilities.description:
## Impact

Some regexes are vulnerable to regular expression denial of service (REDoS) due to overlapping capture groups. This allows remote attackers to overload a server by setting the User-Agent header in an HTTP(S) request to maliciously crafted long strings.

## Patches

Please update uap-core to >= v0.11.0

Downstream packages such as uap-python, uap-ruby etc which depend upon uap-core follow different version schemes.

vulnerabilities.title: uap-core Regular Expression Denial of Service issue
vulnerabilities.description: An issue was discovered in regex.yaml (aka regexes.yaml) in UA-Parser UAP-Core before 0.6.0. A Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) issue allows remote attackers to overload a server by setting the User-Agent header in an HTTP(S) request to a value containing a long digit string. (The UAP-Core project contains the vulnerability, propagating to all implementations.)
